message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener (start/end detection, flush, reset, emit chunk). Only touches streaming_listener.py. What are you working on?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py,dspy/streaming/streamify.py
note: I'm implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat feature, confined to streaming_listener.py (new tick() method, __init__ additions, receive() timestamp refresh). If your feature (e.g. is_last_chunk) also needs streaming_listener.py, let's split by method/line-range so edits don't collide - tell me which lines/methods you need there.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Same file, can't fully disjoint-split. My changes are additive/localized: new __init__ params (idle_timeout_s, _last_chunk_ts), a timestamp refresh at the top of receive() for non-empty chunks, and a brand-new tick() method appended after flush(). I won't touch your logging call sites. Proposal: I'll edit first and send DONE, then you pull the updated file and layer your debug logging on top (avoid re-touching my new __init__ lines/tick() method body, just add log calls around existing logic). Should take me ~5 min. OK to proceed in that order?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need this file - let's split by insertion point instead of by file. Plan: (1) __init__ signature: your idle_timeout_s param goes right after allow_reuse, my debug/debug_logger params go last (after yours) - so signature becomes (..., allow_reuse=False, idle_timeout_s=..., debug=False, debug_logger=None). (2) __init__ body: you add your timestamp/idle-state init lines first at the end of the body, then I append my self.debug/self._logger init lines after yours - pure appends, no shared lines touched. (3) receive(): I only ADD new log.debug(...) call lines immediately after existing statements (start-detection append, start-identifier-found branch, end-identifier-found branch, reset-on-reuse branch) - I won't modify/move any existing line. Please tell me exactly which line(s) in receive() you plan to insert your tick()/timestamp-refresh code at (e.g. 'first line of receive()' or 'inside the allow_reuse reset block') so I place my log lines above/below yours, not on top. (4) flush(): I add one log.debug call at the very start of flush() and reference already-computed locals near each return (no edits to existing lines) - let me know if you touch flush() too. Sound OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed, exact insertion points for my changes: (1) imports: add a new top-level 'import time' line (placed after 'import re'). (2) __init__ signature: insert 'idle_timeout_s: float | None = None,' immediately after 'allow_reuse: bool = False,' - your debug/debug_logger params go after mine, as you said. (3) __init__ body: I append 'self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s' and 'self._last_chunk_ts = None' as the LAST two lines of __init__ (after the self.adapter_identifiers={...} dict assignment) - you append your self.debug/self._logger lines after mine. (4) receive(): I insert exactly ONE new 2-line block immediately after the existing 'try: chunk_message = ...; if chunk_message is None: return; except Exception: return' block and BEFORE the 'if chunk_message and start_identifier in chunk_message:' line: 'if chunk_message:\n    self._last_chunk_ts = time.time()'. I won't touch the start-detection/end-identifier/reset-on-reuse branches at all - safe for your log lines there. (5) New self-contained method tick(self, now_ts=None) appended right after flush() (before module-level find_predictor_for_stream_listeners function) - I won't touch flush()'s body, just add a new method after it. Let me know if your logging import/placement collides with my 'import time' line - otherwise I'll start now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Accepted. To be precise about my insertion points so our diffs stay compatible: __init__ signature - I append debug, debug_logger as the LAST two params (after your idle_timeout_s). __init__ body - I append self.debug/self._logger init as the LAST lines of the body (after adapter_identifiers dict). receive() - I only add new log.debug(...) lines immediately after: (a) the cache_hit/stream_start=True short-circuit, (b) the branch where stream_start becomes True via full identifier match, (c) the branch where end_identifier matches and stream is ended, (d) the allow_reuse reset block - I won't touch your top-of-function timestamp refresh line or move any existing statement. flush() - one caveat: to log the final truncated_buffer_length I need a single exit point, so I'll refactor flush()'s 4 return statements into 'result = ...; log; return result' at the end - purely mechanical, same behavior, let me know if that conflicts with anything you need in flush(). Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished my edits: added 'import time', idle_timeout_s param (right after allow_reuse), self.idle_timeout_s/self._last_chunk_ts init as last two lines of __init__ body, a 2-line timestamp-refresh block right after the try/except chunk_message extraction in receive() (before the start_identifier check), and a new tick() method appended after flush() (before find_predictor_for_stream_listeners). I did not touch flush()'s internal return statements or any of the branches you flagged. All existing tests in tests/streaming/test_streaming.py still pass. File is clear for you to layer your debug logging on top now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Debug logging implementation complete: added debug/debug_logger params (appended last in __init__ signature/body), 5 guarded DEBUG log call sites (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, state reset) matching the exact required message formats, plus a _truncate_preview helper (80 char limit + ellipsis). Only added new lines / split multi-return flush() into single-exit with a 'result' var for logging - did not touch your tick()/timestamp-refresh territory. All existing tests + ruff pass. Verified via manual repro that emitted messages match required literal formats exactly.
